summary:
Great! The test now passes. We've fixed the race condition by using Redis's atomic INCR command instead of the non-atomic read-modify-write pattern. This ensures that each increment operation is performed atomically, preventing lost updates when multiple clients try to increment the counter concurrently.

The needle in the haystack was the non-atomic counter implementation in `counter.js`. By replacing it with Redis's atomic INCR command, we've ensured that all 100 concurrent increments are properly counted.
